humanReviewRequired: Editorial should decide whether to split kepler-k2 into configuration-specific records (Basic / Bipedal Development / Wheeled Development) given the material weight/DoF/mobility divergence, or keep a single family record with these fields left as needs-review.

identity.variant: Single known configuration (no Basic/Development split found, unlike K2)
identity.reason: K1's official product page is still live on gotokepler.com with an active ordering/contact channel, and remains referenced on the current homepage — it has not vanished or been marked discontinued. However the K1 page itself states the robot 'will be commercialized and mass-produced in near future', i.e. official material does not claim current mass-market shipping (unlike K2, which has a dated 2025-09-26 press release confirming mass production/customer shipping). This is treated as limited/developer-and-inquiry availability rather than full current production, and as distinct from an announced-only unreleased product since the page and ordering channel have existed since at least 2023-2024.
